Complete Point Reference — All Exam Points
Point Meridian Exact Location Associated Muscles Clinical Note
KI-27KidneyIn the depression on the lower border of the clavicle, 2 cun lateral to the midlineIliopsoas, Upper Trapezius, Gastrocnemius, Soleus, Tibialis PosteriorThe single most important kidney/adrenal point. Start here for all psoas inhibition and adrenal fatigue pattern. Bilateral stimulation for bilateral findings.
KI-3KidneyMidway between the medial malleolus and Achilles tendon, level with the tip of the malleolusTibialis Posterior, Gastrocnemius, SoleusSource point. Anatomically adjacent to tibialis posterior tendon. Use for arch collapse and adrenal/kidney ankle presentations.
KI-1KidneySole of foot, depression in plantar flexion, junction of anterior 1/3 and posterior 2/3Iliopsoas (adrenal), GastrocnemiusLowest point of body. Use for severe adrenal depletion pattern.
LI-4Large IntestineDorsum of hand, between 1st and 2nd metacarpals, at midpoint of 2nd metacarpal radial sideGluteus Maximus, Hamstrings, TFLHighest yield point in the entire system. Start here for all glute max inhibition and LBP. Contraindicated in pregnancy.
LI-11Large IntestineLateral end of cubital crease when elbow is flexedGluteus Maximus (secondary), HamstringsUse when LI-4 alone does not fully restore glute max. Also excellent for systemic inflammation.
SI-3Small IntestineMake a fist — proximal to 5th metacarpophalangeal joint, at junction of red and white skinGluteus Medius, Gluteus Minimus, Rectus FemorisCommand point of SI meridian. Primary point for Trendelenburg, valgus collapse, and lateral hip pain.
ST-36Stomach3 cun below the lower border of the patella, 1 cun lateral to the tibial crestVMO, Deep Cervical Flexors, Pec Major ClavicularMaster point of the lower limb. Anatomically adjacent to VMO. Most powerful stomach meridian point. Start here for anterior knee and digestive-related inhibition.
CV-12Conception / StomachMidline, 4 cun above the navelVMO, Pec Major Clavicular, Deep Cervical FlexorsAlarm point of the Stomach. Use for VMO inhibition, anterior knee pain, and forward head posture.
CV-6Conception VesselMidline, 2 cun below the navelIliopsoas (adrenal tonification), Gastrocnemius, SoleusTonifies Kidney/Adrenal Qi. Use for adrenal fatigue pattern when multiple KI muscles are inhibited.
SP-3SpleenMedial foot, proximal and inferior to the head of the 1st metatarsal boneMiddle Trapezius, Lower Trapezius, Latissimus DorsiSource point of Spleen meridian. Start here for all trapezius inhibition.
SP-6Spleen3 cun above the medial malleolus, on the posterior border of the tibiaMiddle Trap, Lower Trap, Latissimus (governs SP + LR + KI)Three-yin intersection — governs Spleen, Liver, AND Kidney simultaneously. Use when multiple systems involved.
SP-9SpleenPosterior to the medial condyle of the tibia, in the depression between bone and tendonMiddle Trapezius, Lower TrapeziusSea point of Spleen meridian. Use alongside SP-3 for trapezius inhibition.
LU-1Lung1st intercostal space, 6 cun lateral to midline, in the deltopectoral triangleSerratus Anterior, Anterior Deltoid, CoracobrachialisAlarm point of the Lung. Anatomically adjacent to serratus attachment. Highest yield for shoulder impingement and scapular winging.
LU-7Lung1.5 cun proximal to wrist crease, radial side, just proximal to styloid process of radiusSerratus Anterior (secondary), Anterior DeltoidLuo-connecting point. Use alongside LU-1 for lung pattern. Also command point of Conception Vessel.
HT-7HeartWrist crease, ulnar side — between flexor carpi ulnaris tendon and the pisiform boneSubscapularisSource point of Heart meridian. Primary for subscapularis inhibition and frozen shoulder anterior capsule. Use for IR restriction.
PC-6Pericardium2 cun proximal to wrist crease, between palmaris longus and flexor carpi radialis tendonsSubscapularis (alternate), AdductorsLuo-connecting point. Use for pericardium/emotional component of subscapularis inhibition.
LR-3LiverDorsal foot, in the depression distal to the junction of the 1st and 2nd metatarsal bonesRhomboids (R), Pec Major SternalSource point of Liver meridian. Primary for right-sided body asymmetry. Governs Qi movement and detoxification.
GB-34GallbladderAnterior and inferior to the head of the fibulaRhomboids (left side), PopliteusInfluential point for tendons and sinews. Use for left rhomboid inhibition and lateral knee issues.
BL-40BladderMidpoint of the popliteal crease, between biceps femoris and semitendinosus tendonsPeroneals, Tibialis Anterior, SacrospinalisCommand point of Bladder meridian. Start here for all lateral ankle instability and peroneal inhibition. Remarkable immediate response.
BL-23Bladder1.5 cun lateral to lower border of L2 spinous process (chart shows 1 cun — use 1.5 cun)Peroneals, Tibialis AnteriorBack-shu point of the Kidney. Governs the entire kidney/adrenal system from the posterior. Use alongside KI-27 for adrenal fatigue.
TW-5Triple Warmer2 cun proximal to dorsal wrist crease, between the radius and ulnaInfraspinatus, Teres Minor, SartoriusPrimary for infraspinatus inhibition, ER restriction, and metabolic/thyroid pattern. Use for frozen shoulder posterior component.
GV-20Governing VesselTop of head, on the midline between the earsSupraspinatusGoverns CNS/brain function. Use for supraspinatus inhibition with neurological fatigue.
GV-4Governing VesselBetween the spinous processes of L2 and L3, on the midlineDeep Multifidus, Supraspinatus (secondary)Gate of Life (Ming Men). Governs Yang energy and adrenal function.
LI-20 Large Intestine Beside the nostril, in the nasolabial groove, at the level of the midpoint of the lateral border of the ala nasi Sternocleidomastoid (SCM), Deep Cervical Flexors Terminal point of the Large Intestine meridian. Primary sinus point — stimulate bilaterally and retest SCM and deep cervical flexors immediately. One of the most dramatically immediate clinical responses in the system. Also use for any nasal/sinus congestion presentation accompanying cervical inhibition.
